Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Sooryavanshi termed next 'demigod' of cricket: Srikkanth invokes Tendulkar comparison
Article Text:
Sachin Tendulkar and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi

Watch Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation

‘No need to wait, he’s a match-winner’

Tendulkar comparison raises stakes

Poll Who do you think Vaibhav Sooryavanshi resembles the most in terms of talent? Sachin Tendulkar Virat Kohli MS Dhoni

NEW DELHI: The buzz around Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has reached a fever pitch in IPL 2026, with former India captain Kris Srikkanth making a dramatic call to fast-track the 15-year-old sensation into the national setup — while even rival skipper Shreyas Iyer has labelled him “one for the future.”Srikkanth, known for his blunt takes, did not hold back as he urged selectors to act immediately. “This guy, Sooryavanshi… imagine someone playing such shots. That boy is simply outstanding and brilliant. Ajit, I’m telling you, you need to fast-track him. The next series, he should be playing,” he told The Week, addressing chief selector Ajit Agarkar The former chief selector dismissed the idea of a gradual approach, insisting the youngster is ready for the big stage. “I think Sooryavanshi should be fast-tracked. No question of ‘give him time, let him be consistent’. No chance. That guy can win matches for you just like that,” Srikkanth said.He pointed to the fearless manner in which the teenager has taken on elite bowlers this season — smashing Jasprit Bumrah for sixes and hammering Josh Hazlewood without hesitation. “Imagine, first ball, he hits Bumrah for a six… he’s not bothered about who the bowler is. He’s a prodigy,” he added.In a striking comparison, Srikkanth invoked Sachin Tendulkar ’s early rise. “When I went as captain in 1989, there was a prodigy — a 16-year-old Sachin Tendulkar. Today, he’s the God of Cricket. Similarly, this boy could well become the next ‘demigod’ of cricket,” he said.The excitement is not limited to former players. Punjab Kings skipper Shreyas Iyer also praised the youngster’s technique and temperament. “He is a spectacular talent… the way he has that bat flow, the rhythm and momentum… I think he is one for the future,” Iyer noted.Sooryavanshi has backed the hype with numbers, scoring 246 runs in six matches at a staggering strike rate of 236.53 this season.For now, all eyes remain on the prodigy as Rajasthan Royals face Lucknow Super Giants on Wednesday, with the teenager at the centre of Indian cricket’s next big debate.
Article Summary: The buzz around vaibhav sooryavanshi has reached a fever pitch in ipl 2026. Former india captain kris srikkanth has made a dramatic call to fast-track the 15-year-old sensation. Punjab kings skipper shreyas iyer has labelled him “one for the future’

Article Title: Consistent grit: Women’s 40–49 category shines at Times Internet half marathon
Article Text:
.

Ranjana

New Delhi: The Times Internet Half Marathon saw the women’s 21-kilometre category (40–49 age group) take center stage with stories of enduring dedication and passion for running.Leading the field was Ranjana, 41 years old. Ranjana finished with a chip time of 1:38:59. Her running journey began as early as Class 3, and it’s remained a constant in her life. Growing up in a middle-class family, Ranjana was first motivated by prize money at local races, but over time, running became a deep, lasting passion. She had high praise for the marathon, saying, “jitna accha bolu, kam hai,” when describing the event’s arrangements.Tiffany Stephens secured the first runner-up position with a time of 1:44:44. For Stephens, running is all about consistency and endurance. She sees events like these as chances to push her boundaries and stay connected with the wider running community.Asha Manral finished third in the category, clocking 1:45:18 and adding to the competitive spirit of the race. For Manral, running is a way to maintain both physical and mental balance, and she values the experience of competition as much as the performance itself.The women’s 40–49 group embodied how commitment built over years continues to translate into performance. Every runner brought not just speed, but also a personal story of persistence and growth, inspiring those around them. As they wound their way through Delhi, the event once again went beyond competition, highlighting a shared focus on fitness, discipline, and self-improvement.The Times Internet Half Marathon continues to unite individuals in the spirit of running, where each finish line is more than a result it is a celebration of the journey.
Article Summary: The times internet half marathon saw the women’s 21-kilometre category (40–49 age group) take center stage. Leading the field was ranjana, 41 years old. Every runner brought not just speed, but also a personal story of persistence and growth.

Article Title: Dedication on display: Standout performances in the 40–49 category at Times Internet half marathon
Article Text: At the Times Internet Half Marathon, every category saw brilliance born out of dedication,an event that brought together countless faces, each with a unique story of discipline, effort and passion for running.In the men's 21km (40-49) age category, Pramod Kumar took first place and a chip time of 1:19:26. Kumar has been running for more than ten years and started his running journey as a fitness program in his village and over time developed into a dedication towards becoming more competitive. Kumar also appreciated the overall organization of the event.Naresh Rawat was the first runner-up and finished with a chip time of 1:20:27. Rawat is 46 years old and began running three years ago for fitness and now it has became a lifestyle to keep him committed and stay active.Second runner-up Einar Benediktsson reflected the competitive depth of the category.For Benediktsson, running is a way to stay consistent and push personal limits. He views races as an opportunity to improve while enjoying the experience of the sport.The 40–49 category highlighted how endurance and commitment go beyond age. Participants brought not just performance, but stories of consistency and personal growth. As runners took to the roads of Delhi, the event went beyond competition, showcasing a shared commitment to fitness and perseverance.The Times Internet Half Marathon once again demonstrated how running continues to bring people together, united by the goal of better health and continuous self-improvement.
Article Summary: At the times internet half marathon, every category saw brilliance born out of dedication. Participants brought not just performance, but stories of consistency and personal growth. As runners took to the roads of delhi, the event went beyond competition, showcasing a shared commitment to fitness and perseverance.

Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Youngest and fastest! Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts history, breaks two major IPL records
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (BCCI Photo)

Watch IPL 2026: RR assistant coach Vikram Rathour speaks after loss to KKR IPL 2026: RR assistant coach Vikram Rathour speaks after loss to KKR

Records tumble despite lean outing

Ravindra Jadeja shines as RR bounce back

NEW DELHI: A subdued outing with the bat could not stop Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from scripting history as the teenage sensation rewrote the record books during Rajasthan Royals ’ 40-run win over Lucknow Super Giants in Lucknow on Wednesday.The 15-year-old opener managed just 8 off 11 balls in a low-scoring contest at the Ekana Stadium, but his brief stay proved historic. Sooryavanshi became the youngest player ever to reach 500 IPL runs, surpassing Prithvi Shaw (19 years and 164 days), and also the fastest to the milestone by balls faced, getting there in just 227 deliveries — 33 fewer than previous record-holder Australia’s Glenn Maxwell.At just 15 years and 26 days, Sooryavanshi continues to redefine early success in the IPL. Former RR skipper Sanju Samson (19 years and 195 days) now slips to third on the list of youngest players to 500 runs.Despite Wednesday’s quiet knock, the youngster remains RR’s top scorer this season with 254 runs, including two explosive fifties off just 15 balls each — joint third-fastest in league history.Overall, Sooryavanshi’s numbers underline his impact: 506 runs in 14 matches at an average of 36.14 and a staggering strike rate above 213.His reputation was cemented last season with a 35-ball century against Gujarat Titans — the fastest by an Indian in IPL history and second-fastest overall.While Sooryavanshi broke records, it was Ravindra Jadeja who anchored the victory with a crucial all-round display. His unbeaten 43, combined with a key wicket, earned him the Player of the Match award, which he dedicated to his wife Rivaba Jadeja.Jadeja admitted the pitch was tricky, with seam and swing making strokeplay difficult, prompting him to adopt a patient approach. With the ball, he smartly varied his pace, even joking about having Nicholas Pooran “in his pocket” after outfoxing him.RR captain Riyan Parag felt his side fell slightly short with the bat, suggesting a total closer to 180 was possible. However, the bowlers, led by Jofra Archer and Nandre Burger, ensured the target was more than enough.The victory lifts Rajasthan Royals to second place on the points table, while Lucknow Super Giants remain near the bottom.
Article Summary: Vaibhav sooryavanshi becomes the youngest player ever to reach 500 ipl runs. The 15-year-old opener managed just 8 off 11 balls in a low-scoring contest. Ravindra jadeja anchored the victory with a crucial all-round display.

Article Title: Taylor Swift makes her stance clear on Blake Lively as she and Travis Kelce reportedly send out wedding invites
Article Text:
Taylor Swift makes her stance clear on Blake Lively as she and Travis Kelce reportedly send out wedding invites.(Image via X)

Taylor Swift remains distant from Blake Lively as she plans her wedding to Travis Kelce



Taylor Swift’s ex boyfriend might attend her wedding to Travis Kelce as the pop star reportedly wont invite Blake Lively



Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are all set to host the biggest event of the year as they gear up for their wedding on 3rd July in New York City.As invites are being sent out to their high profile guests, it seems the billionaire pop star has made it clear what she feels about her former best friend, Blake Lively.As per Page Six, a source close to Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce has revealed that the pop star is in no mood to invite Blake Lively to their lavish wedding.The source has revealed that both Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce don't want a “media frenzy” on the day of their wedding, as they reportedly fear Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds’ presence might spark more speculation.As per the US Weekly, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have already sent out invites as a source said, “It won’t be a huge wedding, but there will be many celebrities…”The source added, “They’ve gone back and forth between inviting everyone and keeping it small and private.”As per US Weekly, the source continued, “As of now, they’ve scaled it down. It’s no longer going to be a massive blowout.”However, both Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce remain publicly distant from the rumors about the pop star’s bond with Blake Lively as they focus on their wedding preparations.Interestingly, Taylor Swift’s ex boyfriend, Harry Styles, is rumored to be attending the wedding as his girlfriend, Zoe Kravitz, has been reportedly invited.While Harry Styles has remained silent about Taylor Swift in public, Zoe Kravitz and the pop star have been friends for a long time.A few months ago, as Zoe Kravitz made an appearance on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon as she opened up about Taylor Swift and said, “I’m not surprised because Taylor is scary talented at everything she does. So it’s not surprising that she’s so good.”The popular actress added, “What is weirder to me, actually, is that after she performs for however many thousands of people, she just comes over after and we, like, drink some wine, eat a burger, and hang out…”
Article Summary: Taylor swift makes her stance clear on blake lively as she and travis kelce reportedly send out wedding invites. Source close to taylor swift says the pop star is in no mood to inviteblake lively to their lavish wedding. Harry styles is rumored to be attending the wedding as his girlfriend, zoe kravitz, has been reportedly invited.

Article Title: Amanda Anisimova Net Worth 2026: What is Amanda Anisimova's net worth in 2026? Current salary, endorsements, and rising star journey explored
Article Text:
What is Amanda Anisimova's net worth in 2026? Current salary, endorsements, and rising star journey explored (Image via Getty Images)

Amanda Anisimova’s net worth and early life

Amanda Anisimova’s career growth and major achievements

Amanda Anisimova’s brands and endorsements

Amanda Anisimova’s recent updates and current form

Amanda Anisimova is one of the rising names in women’s tennis. She is known for her strong hitting and calm presence on court. Over the last few years, she has made steady progress and played some big matches at Grand Slams. Fans are now curious about her net worth, career growth, and latest updates as she continues to compete at the top level of the sport.Amanda Anisimova was born on August 31, 2001, in Freehold Township, New Jersey. Her parents moved from Russia to the United States before she was born. Her family later shifted to Florida so she could train better in tennis. She started playing at a very young age after watching her older sister.As per official WTA records and sports financial trackers, Anisimova has earned millions through prize money. Her career prize money is $12.8 million. Her estimated net worth is around $3-4 million as of 2026.Anisimova turned professional in 2016. She quickly became known after reaching the semifinals of the 2019 French Open. In that tournament, she defeated Simona Halep, who was one of the top players at that time.She has won multiple WTA titles, including her first title in Bogota in 2019. Her greatest strength comes from her ability to hit powerful groundstrokes. She has defeated top players throughout her career including Aryna Sabalenka.She achieved a successful 2025 season by reaching the finals of major tournaments. WTA reports confirm her achievement of a top 10 career-high ranking which demonstrates her consistent progress in basketball.Apart from tennis, Anisimova also earns from brand partnerships. She has worked with major global brands like Nike for clothing, shoes and Wilson for racquets. She has also been linked with brands like Gatorade.These deals add a good amount to her total income. Like many top athletes, her brand value is growing as her performance improves on court.Anisimova has maintained her active presence on the WTA Tour through her recent competitions. Throughout the 2026 season, she took part in major tournaments which included both the Australian Open and other important events. Her official match results demonstrate that she advanced to the later stages of multiple tournaments which contributed to her ongoing ranking status.She needed to skip a clay tournament because her wrist injury required her to take time off for recovery. Players use these breaks to sustain their physical condition during the entire competitive season.Amanda Anisimova remains an active, developing player who shows constant improvement. Her current abilities together with her previous experience, will drive her to achieve greater accomplishments throughout the next several years.
Article Summary: Amanda anisimova was born on august 31, 2001, in freehold township, new jersey. Her parents moved from russia to the united states before she was born. Her family later shifted to florida so she could train better in tennis. She started playing at a very young age after watching her older sister.

Article Title: Kavya Maran: IPL 2026: Kavya Maran, Abhishek Sharma’s father go wild after SRH star’s ton — Watch
Article Text:
Kavya Maran and Abhishek's father Raj Kumar Sharma (Video grabs)

Watch Varun Aaron on team performance & key moments Varun Aaron on team performance & key moments

Emotional scenes in the stands go viral

WATCH:

Abhishek leads SRH dominance

Poll Which milestone is more significant in IPL history? Individual centuries Team wins by large margins Record-breaking partnerships Bowling records

NEW DELHI: Abhishek Sharma produced one of the most electrifying knocks of IPL 2026 , but it was the emotional and jubilant scenes in the stands that truly captured the internet’s attention. As the Sunrisers Hyderabad opener brought up his stunning century, cameras panned to a beaming Kavya Maran and Abhishek’s father, Raj Kumar Sharma — both erupting in pure, unfiltered joy.Abhishek’s unbeaten 135 off 68 balls powered SRH to a massive 242/2 against Delhi Capitals, setting up a commanding 47-run win. His knock, laced with 10 fours and 10 sixes, is now among the highest individual scores in IPL history and has quickly become one of the defining innings of the season.The moment Abhishek reached his hundred off just 47 deliveries, the stadium atmosphere hit fever pitch. Kavya Maran’s animated celebrations — cheering, clapping, and visibly overwhelmed — were matched by the pride and excitement of Abhishek’s father, who has been a constant presence in his cricketing journey.The duo’s reactions spread rapidly across social media. The bond between Abhishek and his father added a deeper emotional layer to an already unforgettable innings.On the field, Abhishek was unstoppable. He stitched a 97-run opening stand with Travis Head (37) before accelerating further alongside Heinrich Klaasen, who smashed an unbeaten 37 off 13 balls. Even after being dropped on 86, Abhishek punished Delhi instantly, hammering consecutive sixes to surge past the three-figure mark.“The way we started we had a plan… but the wicket was slow, so we had to adjust,” Abhishek said after the match, underlining the calculated aggression behind his innings.SRH’s dominance continued with the ball as Eshan Malinga picked up 4/32 to restrict DC to 195/9. Delhi skipper Axar Patel admitted his side had little answer. “If someone bats that well… you cannot do anything. You have to move on,” he said.With this win, SRH climbed to third on the table, but the night will be remembered just as much for the emotional celebrations in the stands as for Abhishek’s brilliance with the bat.
Article Summary: Abhishek sharma's unbeaten 135 off 68 balls powered srh to a massive 242/2 against delhi capitals. His knock, laced with 10 fours and 10 sixes, is now among the highest individual scores in ipl history. But it was the emotional and jubilant scenes in the stands that truly captured the internet’s attention.

After nine thrilling matches last week, a trend is beginning to emerge on which teams are on a roll and which are still struggling to settle into the new season. So far, 15 matches have been played in the new season of the IPL , and four teams have already set the early marker in the race for the playoffs.With four wins in four matches, Rajasthan Royals sit at the top of the points table. Punjab Kings are at second after two wins and a no result due to rain. They are followed by defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru, Delhi Capitals and Lucknow Super Giants, who have two wins each so far. Meanwhile, Chennai Super Kings and Kolkata Knight Riders are two winless teams in IPL 2026.With a win each, Sunrisers Hyderabad, Mumbai Indians, and Gujarat Titans have opened their account in the standings and will look to challenge the top four in coming fixtures.The ongoing IPL season has already witnessed multiple weather disruptions across the country. The KKR vs PBKS clash in Kolkata was washed out, while the RR vs MI encounter had to be curtailed to just 11 overs per side in which Rajasthan Royals outclassed Mumbai Indians by 27 runs. And on Wednesday, GT held their nerves to beat hosts DC by one run in a last-ball thriller.KKR lost their 3rd match in a row after Mukul Choudhary pulled off a record breaking chase for LSG in the last-ball thriller.Rajasthan Royals and Royal Challengers Bengaluru, face off in Guwahati on Friday.The weekend double-headers will see Punjab Kings take on Sunrisers Hyderabad and Chennai Super Kings face Delhi Capitals on Saturday, followed by Lucknow Super Giants vs Gujarat Titans and Mumbai Indians vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Sunday.There is likely to be plenty of shuffling in the points table this week.
Article Summary: Rajasthan royals and royal challengers bengaluru, face off in guwahati on friday. Chennai super kings and kolkata knight riders are two winless teams in ipl 2026. Sunrisers hyderabad, mumbai indians, and gujarat titans have opened their account in the standings.

Article Title: Ipl 2026: IPL 2026: Will DC make changes to XI after defeat to SRH? Skipper Axar Patel clarifies
Article Text:
SRH vs DC

Watch Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation Ashok Sharma story: Inside the village and house which shaped India's new pace sensation

NEW DELHI: Sunrisers Hyderabad’s dominant win over Delhi Capitals was once again defined by Abhishek Sharma ’s explosive batting and a disciplined bowling effort led by Eshan Malinga. However, DC skipper Axar Patel remained calm after the defeat, suggesting no immediate panic or changes to the XI despite the heavy loss.Speaking after the match, Axar backed his squad and stressed that the team’s overall process was still on track. He said, "No, right now it doesn’t feel like that, because the kind of cricket that is going on - if you see, obviously we are batting well and bowling well. It feels like, I think you know, when the wicket is so good, then this should be considered an off day and forgotten."He also acknowledged missed opportunities in the field that hurt DC’s chances against Abhishek’s onslaught. "The run-out and the catch we missed - had we taken it, we could have restricted them," he said, while still giving full credit to SRH’s batting firepower.Axar further added that even good execution sometimes fails against extraordinary batting. "If someone bats that well, and their execution is not lacking, credit must be given for such a performance... when a batsman bats like this, the coach and the captain must accept that even a captain cannot stop someone," he explained.He also emphasised the importance of support within the team environment during tough games, particularly for bowlers under pressure.The match itself saw Abhishek Sharma produce a breathtaking unbeaten 135 off 68 balls, powering SRH to a massive 242/2. Despite a brief fightback from Delhi’s middle order, Malinga’s 4/32 broke the chase apart as DC were restricted to 195/9.Abhishek’s knock, supported by Travis Head and Heinrich Klaasen, ensured SRH’s dominance from the start, while Axar admitted that sometimes, opponents simply outplay you on the day.
Article Summary: Sunrisers hyderabad beat delhi capitals by eight wickets. Abhishek sharma top scored for srh with 135 off 68 balls. Eshan malinga took 4/32 as dc were restricted to 195/9. Dc captain axar patel remained calm after the defeat.

Article Title: A.J. Brown Trade Rumors: C.J. Gardner-Johnson takes to X to bash Philadelphia Eagles fans for backstabbing A.J. Brown amid trade buzz
Article Text:
(Image via Getty)

A.J. Brown trade buzz draws strong response from CJ Gardner-Johnson



"Philly turned on AJ so bad it’s sad! Yall love us when we help yall win but damn why yall bashing Bruda now?!"

Philadelphia Eagles fans are famous for being some of the loudest and most passionate people in all of sports. They love their team with all their hearts, but when things get a little shaky, they aren't afraid to let everyone know exactly how they feel. Right now, the internet is buzzing with some drama involving star wide receiver A.J. Brown.Lately, there has been a lot of talk and rumors suggesting the Eagles might be thinking about trading A.J. Brown. In the NFL, rumors travel fast, and often before anyone really knows what is happening, fans start to react. Some fans have started criticizing Brown, questioning if he is still the right player for the team.This chatter hasn't gone unnoticed by the other players in the league. C.J. Gardner-Johnson, also known as CJGJ, decided he had seen enough of the negativity. He took to social media to call out the Philadelphia Eagles for how they are treating his friend and former teammate.CJGJ kept it simple and direct in his post:Essentially, CJGJ is telling the fans to show some loyalty. He feels it is unfair for fans to shower players with love when the team is winning and everything is going great, only to turn their backs the moment a rumor pops up.He calls A.J. his "Bruda," a way of saying he sees him like a brother. In the NFL, players form very strong bonds because they go to war on the field together every single Sunday.It is a classic example of the "What have you done for me lately?" attitude that often happens in big cities with huge sports expectations. Athletes hear what people are saying on social media, and sometimes, a friend like CJGJ just has to stand up and say, "Hey, give him a break!"Whether or not the Eagles actually trade A.J. Brown remains a mystery. We are right in the middle of the busy pre-draft season, so anything is possible. But one thing is very clear: the players are going to stick up for each other.It is a good reminder that behind the helmets and the jerseys, these are real people with real feelings. Maybe it is time for the Eagles to take a deep breath, support their players, and wait to see what actually happens. After all, football is a rollercoaster, and it is usually more fun when everyone is cheering for the same team!
Article Summary: C.j. Gardner-johnson took to social media to call out the philadelphia eagles for how they are treating his friend and former teammate. He feels it is unfair for fans to shower players with love when the team is winning and everything is going great, only to turn their backs the moment a rumor pops up.

Article Title: Aryna Sabalenka fiancé Georgios Frangulis addresses critics questioning his presence during her tennis matches
Article Text:
Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis (Image Via Getty)

Money Buys Happiness podcast



Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis relationship and response to criticism explained

Esquire

There has been a lot of talk around Aryna Sabalenka and her fiancé Georgios Frangulis. Many fans have noticed him sitting in the stands during her matches, and not everyone has been supportive. Some people even questioned how he manages his business while attending so many games. Now, Georgios has finally responded, and his message was very direct.In a video shared by theon Instagram, Georgios made it clear that he is not bothered by the criticism. He spoke openly about his relationship and how they support each other. At the same time, he also pushed back at people who keep judging his choices and his presence during Sabalenka’s matches.Aryna Sabalenka and Georgios Frangulis started dating in 2024, and earlier this year, they announced their engagement. Since then, Georgios has often been seen in her player box during big matches. While many fans enjoy seeing that support, others have raised questions about his work and time.Georgios Frangulis addressed this directly in his statement. He said, “For me to have someone that I love that trusts me and looks up to me.I can teach and learn from, with my fiancé. With Aryna it’s all upside. I don’t see any downside.”He then spoke about the criticism he receives and how he handles his work. He explained that watching matches does not stop him from managing his responsibilities. He said, “Believe me I get criticism. How can he run the company. He’s always watching tennis. You see me on TV and I am watching tennis. It’s a two-hour match. Like I was doing a meeting before I’m gonna do another after. Not your business what I am doing.”Georgios Frangulis also praised Aryna Sabalenka’s success and their shared understanding. He added, “She’s super accomplished. We can enjoy each other’s success, help each other’s failures.”On the other side, Sabalenka has also spoken about how important this relationship is for her. In an interview with, she shared a simple lesson she has learned. She said, “You see that each area of life takes the same dedication, discipline, and hard work.”While all this is happening off the court, Sabalenka’s form on the court has been strong. She started the season by winning the Brisbane International and then reached the final of the Australian Open. She later completed the Sunshine Double by winning both Indian Wells and the Miami Open, beating top players like Elena Rybakina and Coco Gauff.She was set to play at the Stuttgart Open but pulled out due to an injury picked up in Miami. Even so, both her performance and her personal life continue to stay in the spotlight.
Article Summary: Aryna sabalenka and georgios frangulis started dating in 2024. Georgios has often been seen in her player box during big matches. While many fans enjoy seeing that support, others have raised questions about his work and time.

Article Title: Toronto Maple Leafs forward Matthew Knies emerges as Chicago Blackhawks target in latest NHL trade rumors
Article Text:
Matthew Knies' trade rumors begin to grow amidst disappointing 2025-26 campaign (Image via Getty Images)

Watch Matthew Knies | End of Season Media Availability | April 16, 2026 Matthew Knies | End of Season Media Availability | April 16, 2026

Even though Chicago is rebuilding, they have looked into adding established young players in the past instead of just relying on draft picks.From Toronto's point of view, it wouldn't be easy to move Knies. Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El rubius was eliminated from mrbeast's $1 million challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal challengers bengaluru (rcb) beat chennai super kings (csk) by 43 runs. Tim david, rajat patidar and devdutt padikkal all scored half-centuries. Rcb set a new record for highest total in ipl 2026 with 250/3.

Article Title: Shattering records and cultivating future stars
Article Text:
Mayura Shanbaug

Ashish Kumar Srivastava - MD and CEO of PNB MetLife

Sameer Bansal - Chief Distribution Officer of PNB MetLife

Chirag Shetty - Asian Games Gold Medalist & JBC 2023 mentor

Satwiksairaj Rankireddy - Asian Games Gold Medalist & JBC 2023 mentor

Disclaimer: Content Produced by PNB Metlife

The 7th PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship shattered records with over 8,200 young talent nationwide, earning global recognition and fostering a culture of resilience and determination in aspiring badminton starsIn a spectacular display of badminton prowess and youthful enthusiasm, the 7th edition of the PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship (JBC) etched its name in the annals of sporting history. Celebrating a staggering participation of 8257 young champions from across India, the championship not only broke its own record set in 2022 but also clinched the title of the ‘Most kids participating in a Badminton Championship in multiple cities’ as recognised by the World Record Certification Agency (WRCA).The grand finale unfolded at the Indira Gandhi Indoor Stadium in New Delhi, where the air was thick with competition, camaraderie, and the unmistakable passion for badminton. The event, graced by chief guests Chirag Shetty and Satwik Rankireddy, Asian Games Gold Medalists and JBC 2023 mentors, was a true celebration of the sport’s rising stars.The 10-city extravaganza, spanning the length and breadth of the country, showcased the unwavering commitment of PNB MetLife in nurturing young talents in the badminton arena. With participants exceeding 8200, it’s evident that the PNB MetLife JBC has become a beacon for aspiring shuttlers, highlighting the deep-seated passion for badminton among the diverse and talented youth of India.Beyond the glitz and glamour of the record-breaking numbers, the championship holds a special place in the hearts of those at the helm. Ashish Kumar Srivastava, MD and CEO of PNB MetLife, expressed pride in not just surpassing their own world record but also in contributing to India’s glory on the international badminton stage. The championship, he emphasized, is not merely a competition but a significant milestone in creating a pan-India badminton ecosystem.This edition of the JBC saw a strategic partnership with world champions Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, who served as mentors for the young talents. Sameer Bansal, Chief Distribution Officer of PNB MetLife, acknowledged the pivotal role played by these champions in inspiring the next generation of badminton stars. The focus, according to Bansal, is on unleashing the latent potential of young shuttlers and preparing them for global excellence.The Delhi chapter of the championship witnessed some nail-biting action with over 1000 participants competing fiercely across various categories. From Ananya Talyan’s stellar performance in Girls Singles Under 9 to Divyansh Rawat’s triumph in Boys Singles Under 17, the young talents left no shuttle unturned in their quest for victory.The results from the Delhi leg not only showcased the raw talent but also highlighted the championship’s commitment to breaking barriers. The PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship is not just about records; it’s about fostering a culture of healthy living, determination, and resilience among the future champions of the sport.In the words of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, the championship serves as an invaluable platform.---------------------------------------------------------------As we celebrate the successful culmination of the 7th edition of PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship , we take pride in surpassing our own world record for highest participation and for being recognized by World Record Certification Agency. Beyond the records, JBC represents a significant milestone in our mission to lay the groundwork for India’ s glory and success on the international badminton stage by creating a pan-India badminton ecosystem that nurtures champions at the grassroots levelThis year has been especially remarkable for PNB MetLife JBC as we joined hands with world champions Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy as JBC Mentors for the young talents associated with this tournament. They have played a pivotal role in inspiring young shuttlers to dream big and elevate their game and skills. We are eager for the next edition to unfold in our journey of unveiling the latent badminton potential across our nation and preparing them for global excellenceIt’s inspiring to witness the boundless talent and enthusiasm within our young athletes. My journey as JBC mentor has been incredibly fulfilling, as I got the opportunity to guide these budding champions and witness the journey of these young badminton stars. This championship is more than a competition; it is a platform that nurtures dreams, ignites aspirations, and, I believe, it is laying the foundation for future gold medalists in the makingTournaments like these serve as an invaluable platform for nurturing and harnessing the potential of our country’s young badminton stars. Witnessing the enthusiasm, passion, and skill displayed by these emerging shuttlers reinforces the belief that our country has an immense reservoir of talent. My association as a JBC mentor has been a source of immense pride, particularly given the role that the championship is playing in creating an incredible platform that is serving as a powerful catalyst, empowering young players and bolstering their confidence to become future champions
Article Summary: The 7th pnb metlife junior badminton championship shattered records with over 8,200 young talent nationwide. The grand finale unfolded at the indira gandhi indoor stadium in new delhi, where the air was thick with competition, camaraderie, and the unmistakable passion for badminton.

Article Title: IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dismissed for 8 as RR’s top order falls apart vs LSG
Article Text:
Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (ANI Photo)

Watch IPL 2026: RR assistant coach Vikram Rathour speaks after loss to KKR IPL 2026: RR assistant coach Vikram Rathour speaks after loss to KKR

The spotlight was firmly on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi heading into Rajasthan Royals ’ clash against Lucknow Super Giants, with Sooryavanshi coming into the game in red-hot form. But on this occasion, the teenage sensation endured a rare failure.Sooryavanshi managed just 8 runs off 11 balls, unable to find the rhythm that has defined his tournament so far. It was part of a dramatic early collapse for Rajasthan Royals, whose top order, responsible for the bulk of their runs this season, was dismantled inside the powerplay.The damage began in the third over when Mohammed Shami struck twice in consecutive deliveries, removing Yashasvi Jaiswal and Dhruv Jurel to leave Rajasthan reeling early.Sooryavanshi’s dismissal followed soon after, and it was one he would look back on with regret. On the final ball of the fourth over, Mohsin Khan bowled a length delivery outside off. Attempting to go big, Sooryavanshi lost his shape and mistimed the shot, toe-ending it towards cover. Digvesh Singh Rathi then produced a superb running catch, tracking back as the ball swirled in the air before completing it with ease.With the top three back in the dugout early, the responsibility now shifts to captain Riyan Parag and Shimron Hetmyer , both of whom have struggled for consistency this season and are yet to make a significant impact.Earlier in the evening at the Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow, Rishabh Pant won the toss and opted to field first in Match 32 of IPL 2026. Lucknow made two changes, bringing in Digvesh Rathi and Mayank Yadav, with M Siddharth and Avesh Khan making way.Both teams entered the contest under pressure. Lucknow were on a three-match losing streak and are yet to register a home win this season, while Rajasthan came in after back-to-back defeats and are still searching for their first away victory.On a night when expectations were high around Sooryavanshi, it turned into a rare off day, one that has left Rajasthan’s middle order with a big task ahead.
Article Summary: Sooryavanshi managed just 8 runs off 11 balls, unable to find the rhythm that has defined his tournament so far. Mohammed shami struck twice in consecutive deliveries, removing yashasvi jaiswal and dhruv jurel to leave rajasthan reeling early.

Article Title: NFL insider explains Mike Vrabel’s first press conference after alleged Sedona cheating scandal with Dianna Russini
Article Text:
NFL insider explains Mike Vrabel’s first press conference after alleged Sedona cheating scandal with Dianna Russini (Image via Getty: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini)

Albert Breer sheds light on Mike Vrabel’s media session after alleged photos with Dianna Russini surfaced online

New England Patriots head coach promises the best version of himself going forward

Body language expert analyzed Mike Vrabel

The three weeks leading up to the 2026 NFL Draft have been difficult for New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel. Ever since his alleged photos with top NFL reporter Dianna Russini at the Sedona hotel broke internet records.There were rumors that the coach would address the press during the draft, but in the crucial days leading up to D-day, his players were scheduled to address the media. So, the coach didn’t think it’d be right for them to address the media before he did. What if they sprang questions about him before his team? [hypothesis].“Stacy [James, Patriots VP of Media Relations] had mentioned the players’ availability today,” Vrabel said at the unannounced press conference. “And it was never my intention to have them speak to you or address you before I did. I also don’t want to take away from the draft, the weekend of the draft.”After a two-minute-plus statement, the coach held a side session with reporters, but they were ordered to turn off all cameras, including phones, and keep questions strictly football-related. As for decoding the coach’s first unannounced press conference, an NFL insider has stepped up to shed light on it.Let’s take a look.Sports Illustrated’s Albert Breer shared an article titled, “Latest NFL Draft Buzz: Comparing the Top Wide Receivers,” and it included a former Patriots linebacker, aka Mike Vrabel.The NFL insider called it “one final non-draft note.” Breer said that Vrabel first wanted to talk to his players before he addressed the media. He spoke to his players during the offseason program on Monday.He reportedly apologized to the players and the organization for diverting unwanted attention to the controversy. He promised he would do everything to make it go away and bring the spotlight back on them.Breer said that those who heard the coach in the room may seem to take him for genuine, remorseful, and, to some extent, relatable.The analyst further said, “His personal life is his personal life and, obviously, he’ll have to deal with that part of it. Professionally, Vrabel has preached accountability to his players, the same way most coaches do.”Vrabel had some difficult conversations with the people he cares about: his family, the Patriots organization, and his players. The conversations have been positive and productive.They all believe that to be successful on and off the gridiron, they need to make good decisions. And this thought begins with him. They never want their actions to harm the team. They never want to be the cause of a distraction.He further said, “Those are comments and questions that I’ve answered for the team, and with the team, we’ll keep those private and to ourselves.” He cares deeply about the Patriots team and is excited to coach them.He will attack each day with humility and focus. And lastly, he promises to be the best version of himself going forward for his family, the organization, the team, the staff, the coaches, everybody, most importantly, the Patriots fans.Bottomline: the coach chose not to confirm or deny any claims tied to the alleged Sedona cheating scandal.Page Six’s Leah Bitsky and Audrey Rock were informed by body language expert Susan Constantine that during the press meet, Mike Vrabel was constantly “rocking from side-to-side and feeling discomfort” while addressing the press. The expert spotted “genuine sadness” in Vrabel’s eyes.The coach was “truthful and sincere” during the meet. Constantine noticed he pressed his lips together, which signals “containment of emotions,” while his vocal pitch “punctuated key points.” There were no untruthful indicators, but the coach did appear to be “uncomfortable and ill at ease.”Speculation alert: By apologizing for the unwanted attention, did the coach admit to those photos? Regarding body language, the judgment and scrutiny surrounding the photos likely caused the coach to experience those intense emotions. Plus, was the press meet a form of damage control?
Article Summary: Nfl insider explains mike vrabel’s first press conference after alleged sedona cheating scandal with dianna russini. Body language expert analyzed mike v rabel during the press meet. The coach was constantly “rocking from side-to-side and feeling discomfort” while addressing the press.

Article Title: The Adidas Hyperboost Edge will make you actually want to go outside
Article Text:
The Adidas Hyperboost Edge is not your old Boost. The foam is new, the feel is different, and the ride is better than anything Adidas has put in a training shoe in years. The upper breathes about as well as a sealed envelope—a real problem in an Indian summer—but the Hyperboost Pro midsole makes a convincing case for itself the moment you pick up the pace. At Rs 19,999, it mostly justifies the ask.

This is not your old Boost

The foam has a second gear, and it takes a kilometre to find it

The upper looks the part and mostly plays it

For someone who only runs when they have to, this makes the running better





I don't really run. I mean, I do—but not in the way runners run. I don't have a training plan, I don't own a GPS watch, and I couldn't tell you what my VO2 max is. What I do have is a habit of lacing up whenever a bad day gets bad enough that sitting around feels worse than sweating through it. Running, for me, is less a fitness pursuit and more an anger management strategy. Cheaper than therapy, arguably more effective.So when I say the Hyperboost Edge is a good shoe, I mean it in the most practical sense possible: it made the thing I do when I'm miserable feel noticeably less miserable. That's not nothing. And given that this shoe costs Rs 19,999, it better be worth leaving the house for. It is, mostly. But nothing about running shoes is ever that simple.It starts, as most things do with running shoes, with what's underfoot—a foam Adidas calls Hyperboost Pro. The name is doing a lot of work here, and it's not quite what it sounds like.Hyperboost Pro is not Boost. It looks like Boost—same pelletized, bead-like construction, same visual texture—but the material underneath is completely different. Classic Boost was TPU, which was great in 2013 and increasingly ordinary by 2023.Hyperboost Pro is ePEBA, which is the same foam family Adidas put inside the Adios Pro Evo, a racing shoe so light it borders on irresponsible. What Adidas has done here is take that material and make it last.ePEBA is lighter, snappier, and doesn't have that dense, slightly reluctant feel old Boost had, the kind where the foam felt like it needed a moment to wake up. The beads look the same. That's the only thing that does.How it's made matters as much as what it's made of. Most PEBA foams are solid slabs, great for race day, not built for the kind of mileage you'd put a daily trainer through. The pelletised version here trades a little of that rawness for something more practical. It stays consistent. It doesn't feel different after fifty kilometres than it did after five. And it doesn't ask you to save it for special occasions, which for a shoe you're meant to run in regularly, is sort of the whole point.Independent lab testing puts energy return at 73.6%, which without turning this into a chemistry lecture simply means it outperforms most of what's in this category. It also stiffens by only 9% in cold conditions—most foams stiffen by 23%. Impressive, if numbers do it for you. But the more convincing argument is what it actually feels like to run in. That's a better conversation.At easy paces, the Hyperboost Edge is the kind of comfortable that makes you slightly suspicious. The 45mm stack absorbs everything. Road? Gone. Impact? Handled. There's essentially zero ground feel, which is either the whole point or a dealbreaker, depending on your persuasion. For someone who runs to stop thinking rather than to feel every footstrike, it's exactly right.Easy paces are one thing. Pick up the pace and the foam wakes up. The Hyperboost Pro midsole, which feels almost passive at a casual jog, starts doing something noticeably different when you load it properly. It pushes back. Not aggressively—but enough that you feel like the shoe is participating rather than just sitting there. Like it has a stake in where your foot lands.The forefoot rocker, late stage and slightly aggressive, adds to this. It encourages a snappy toe-off that feels less like geometry and more like the shoe nudging you forward. The Lighttraxion outsole keeps up, grippy enough on pavement that traction never enters your head. At a pace where you're actually working, the whole thing clicks into place in a way that's genuinely satisfying.That responsiveness at effort is what separates genuinely good cushioned trainers from ones that just have a lot of foam in them—and the Hyperboost Edge lands clearly on the right side of that line. Though not everyone who ran in these would agree.A cousin of mine borrowed these for a week and came back the next day—mostly to complain about his shins. He heel strikes, and after his first run he wouldn't stop going on about them. The heel geometry is the culprit—at 45mm of stack, you really need a properly bevelled heel to ease the transition, and the Hyperboost Edge doesn't quite deliver that. Without it, the shoe hits the ground earlier than it should, and the shin muscles end up absorbing more than they bargained for.I run forefoot so I never felt any of it, the shoe suits that strike pattern considerably better. If you heel strike, try these on before buying. The shins don't lie.What I was actually more worried about was getting the shoes back at all, but the shin situation settled that quickly. They came back, and barely used at that. No visible outsole wear, no foam compression after serious mileage. Which, honestly, was the more reassuring thing to come out of that week.The Primeweave upper is, aesthetically, one of the more confident things Adidas has done in a while. The three stripes sit on the midsole stack rather than the upper, a small decision that makes the shoe look like it knows exactly what it is. On foot, the lockdown is genuinely impressive. The gusseted tongue doesn't budge, the split heel counter cradles the Achilles without pressing into it—a detail that runners with sensitive tendons will quietly appreciate—and the whole thing feels more premium than most shoes at this price. It's a well-constructed upper. It just has one significant problem.Summer. The dense woven mesh, the same thing making it look so good, breathes about as well as a car parked in the sun. Adidas apparently has a more ventilated version coming, which is great news delivered at the wrong time if you're buying now and planning to run through April in Delhi. The lacing doesn't help either: thin, hidden inside the upper, looks clever, takes three adjustments to get right. It's the kind of thing you figure out eventually and then stop thinking about, but you shouldn't have to figure it out at all.Rs 19,999 is a number that demands a shoe justify itself, and the Hyperboost Edge mostly does. The upper needs work, and in an Indian summer it needs it badly. But the foam is a different thing entirely—lighter, more responsive, and noticeably unlike anything the Boost name used to mean. You feel that the moment the pace picks up.There's also something to be said for a shoe that looks good enough that you don't immediately want to hide it in a bag after your run. The Hyperboost Edge is bold in a way that either works for you or doesn't, the chunky midsole, the clean upper, the stripes sitting low, but it commits to that boldness rather than stumbling into it.Warm upper and all, it's still the most I've enjoyed going for a run in a while. For someone whose bar is simply "make the bad day slightly more bearable," that's a reasonable endorsement.
Article Summary: Adidas hyperboost edge is not your old boost. The foam is new, the feel is different, and the ride is better than anything adidas has put in a training shoe in years. The upper breathes about as well as a sealed envelope. At rs 19,999, it mostly justifies the ask.
